level_signals: 8+ years of experience in equity administration, stock plan management, or related field
Compensation range $190K-$230K base, consistent with Senior IC at Anthropic

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in equity administration, stock plan management, or related field
Strong knowledge of equity compensation principles (stock options, RSUs)
Experience with Shareworks equity management platforms
Proficiency in payroll systems integration and tax withholding processes
Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication skills with ability to explain complex equity concepts to employees
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience at high-growth technology companies during scaling phases
Knowledge of US and international equity compensation and tax requirements
Excellent project management skills with a track record of leading complex, cross-functional initiatives
Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills and experience with modeling

scope.surface: Global equity compensation programs across all markets
scope.business_model: public benefit corporation, AI safety company
primary_metrics_inferred: Accuracy and timeliness of equity grant processing and reconciliation
Compliance with global tax and regulatory requirements
Employee satisfaction with equity program support
Scalability of equity operations for headcount growth
core_responsibilities: Oversee daily equity plan operations including grant processing, option exercises, cancellation and cap table management
Build and optimize processes for equity transactions while ensuring accuracy and compliance
Manage monthly equity reconciliations and reporting
Lead demographic and payroll system integrations to streamline equity operations
Develop and implement automated processes for tax rate calculations and payroll integration
Create scalable workflows that can support our growing headcount and global expansion
Provide high-touch service to ensure employees understand their equity compensation
Execute simple equity tax calculations in coordination with payroll processing
Ensure timely and accurate tax withholding and reporting for equity transactions
Support development of equity tax policies and procedures
Design and document standard operating procedures for all equity operations
Create scalable processes that will support transition to public company requirements
Provide guidance and support to managers and employees on equity-related questions and programs

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($190K-$230K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with reasonable effort commitment
Clear and specific scope of responsibilities — not vague
Role is operationally important as Anthropic prepares for potential public company transition
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
red_flags: CEP designation listed under 'You may be a good fit if you have' — language is softer than a hard requirement, but its specificity makes it effectively a strong filter
Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Role involves high operational complexity (global tax, payroll integration, cap table) but team size and reporting structure are unstated
Public company transition readiness is a stated goal, implying significant near-term workload increase

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + title)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from JD description of Anthropic as public benefit corporation)
location.cities_listed (San Francisco inferred from HQ mention; no other cities named for this role)
ambiguities: CEP designation appears under 'You may be a good fit if you have' (softer language) rather than a hard 'Required' section, yet it is a specific professional credential. Classified as hard severity given its specificity and industry norms for senior equity roles, but flagged here.

The 'You may be a good fit if you have' section uses conditional language for all items including the 8+ years experience requirement. All items in that section have been treated as must_haves (hard) given their unconditional framing within the section and the seniority of the role. Items in 'Strong candidates may have' treated as nice_to_haves (soft).
